title: Epic: Phase 6 — Polish + release
description:
Shell completions, tracing wiring, color polish, cargo-dist 0.1.0 release, README install instructions, cutover from Python ~/bin/grove to ~/.cargo/bin/grove.

See: 00-master-plan.md (Phase 6), 04-launch-test-ci.md

title: Shell completions via clap_complete
description:
## Context
Generate shell completions for fish, bash, zsh, pwsh via clap_complete. Wired through a hidden __completions subcommand.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** grove __completions fish, **When** run, **Then** outputs a valid fish completion script to stdout (parseable by fish -c 'source -').
- **Given** the same for bash, zsh, pwsh, **When** run, **Then** outputs the corresponding valid completion script.
- **Given** completions for subcommands, **When** generated, **Then** they cover all subcommand-scoped flags (not just top-level).

## Files
- src/cli/completions.rs — hidden subcommand handler invoking clap_complete::generate.

## Test Requirements
- Integration: 4 tests asserting non-empty output and presence of subcommand names in each shells output.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-4i5.8

## Notes
02-cli-ux.md completions section. Install location is documented in README; not auto-installed.

title: tracing-subscriber wiring (-v, -vv, RUST_LOG)
description:
## Context
Structured logging via tracing. -v sets level to INFO, -vv to DEBUG; RUST_LOG env var overrides everything.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** default invocation, **When** grove runs, **Then** WARN and above are logged (no chatter).
- **Given** -v, **When** invoked, **Then** INFO and above appear.
- **Given** -vv, **When** invoked, **Then** DEBUG and above appear.
- **Given** RUST_LOG=grove::git=trace, **When** invoked, **Then** that module logs at TRACE regardless of -v.
- **Given** non-TTY stderr, **When** logging, **Then** no ANSI color codes are emitted to stderr.

## Files
- src/main.rs — install tracing-subscriber early.

## Test Requirements
- Unit: 2 tests via the test-log crate or manual capture.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-fut.1

## Notes
04-launch-test-ci.md section 7.

title: Color polish, NO_COLOR, table width
description:
## Context
Final pass on visual polish: NO_COLOR support, table width auto-detection, dim styling for frozen projects, status color coding consistent.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** NO_COLOR=1, **When** any command runs, **Then** no ANSI color codes appear in any output (stdout or stderr).
- **Given** stdout is not a TTY, **When** running grove list, **Then** colors are disabled automatically (is-terminal check).
- **Given** terminal width 80 cols, **When** rendering a table, **Then** the table fits without wrapping unexpected columns (truncate or stack as needed).
- **Given** a frozen project in grove list, **When** rendered, **Then** the tag is shown in dim style.

## Files
- src/display.rs — color and width helpers; central NO_COLOR check.

## Test Requirements
- Integration: 4 tests covering NO_COLOR, pipe redirect, narrow terminal, frozen styling.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-rez.3

## Notes
02-cli-ux.md polish section.

title: cargo-dist 0.1.0 release + README + cutover
description:
## Context
Final release: tag v0.1.0, cargo-dist builds artifacts for 5 targets, GitHub Releases hosts them, cargo-binstall finds them, README documents install path. Cutover from Python ~/bin/grove.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** a v0.1.0 tag pushed to origin, **When** the release workflow runs, **Then** artifacts for linux-x86_64, linux-aarch64, macos-x86_64, macos-arm64, windows-x86_64 are uploaded with checksums + a dist-manifest.json.
- **Given** the release exists, **When** cargo binstall grove is run on any supported target, **Then** the correct artifact is downloaded and installed to the cargo bin directory.
- **Given** README, **When** viewed on GitHub, **Then** it includes install instructions (cargo binstall primary, cargo install fallback, curl-installer alternate), brief usage examples, and link to the Phase 0 plan.
- **Given** the new binary on Oysteins machine, **When** grove --version is run, **Then** outputs version 0.1.0 with git sha + build date.
- **Given** cutover, **When** Python ~/bin/grove is removed, **Then** ~/.cargo/bin/grove (ahead on PATH) handles all commands identically.

## Files
- README.md — install + usage.
- src/main.rs — --version include git sha (via vergen or build.rs).
- Migration of old script: rename to grove.py in dotfiles as reference, remove ~/bin/grove symlink.

## Test Requirements
- Manual: confirm release workflow succeeds end to end on a v0.0.0-rc test tag before cutting v0.1.0.
- Manual: full lifecycle on Oysteins machine: install, run grove list, run grove launch --dry-run, confirm parity with Python output.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-4i5.8, grove-u6c.5, grove-1bt.1, grove-1bt.2, grove-1bt.3, grove-rez.8, grove-4pe.2, grove-4pe.3, grove-4pe.4, grove-ktk.6

## Notes
04-launch-test-ci.md section 6. cargo-dist quickstart at https://opensource.axo.dev/cargo-dist/.

title: Epic: Phase 3 — Worktree mutations
description:
Implement new, fork, adopt, rename, freeze, thaw, done with WorktreeManager trait (gix for inspection, shell-out for add/remove/move). Integration tests via GitFixture helper.

See: 00-master-plan.md (Phase 3), 03-git-internals.md

title: Shell backend for worktree add/remove/move
description:
## Context
gix does not yet support worktree add/remove/move natively. Shell out to `git worktree` for these three operations. Trait abstraction allows swap when gix lands native API (issue #2596).

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** a target path and branch, **When** ShellBackend::worktree_add runs, **Then** it invokes `git worktree add <path> <branch>` and returns Ok on exit 0.
- **Given** a worktree path, **When** worktree_remove runs, **Then** invokes `git worktree remove <path>` (or `--force` when force=true) and returns Ok on success.
- **Given** old + new path, **When** worktree_move runs, **Then** invokes `git worktree move <old> <new>`.
- **Given** stderr from git, **When** the subprocess fails, **Then** the error captures stderr and is returned as GroveError::GitCommandFailed{cmd, stderr}.
- **Given** WSL with /c/ paths, **When** invoking git, **Then** paths are passed as-is (git accepts both forms under WSL) — but the test verifies no double-conversion.

## Files
- src/git/shell_backend.rs — ShellBackend struct implementing WorktreeManager mutation methods.

## Test Requirements
- Integration: 4 tests (add, remove, remove --force, move) against tempdir repo.
- Error path: invoke against missing repo, assert structured error.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-rez.1

## Notes
03-git-internals.md section 2. Use std::process::Command. Inherit env (so git credential helper works). Stream stderr into the error.

title: grove new command
description:
## Context
`grove new <tag> [--issue N] [--branch name] [--base ref] [--no-fetch]` creates a new worktree, branch, and registers the project.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** `grove new lazy-vm --issue 9947`, **When** run inside the desktop repo context, **Then** a branch `DESKTOP-9947-lazy-vm` is created from `if/master`, a worktree is added at `<work_dir>/lazy-vm`, and the project is recorded in the registry.
- **Given** no --issue and no --branch, **When** run, **Then** branch name equals tag.
- **Given** --branch overrides --issue, **When** both passed, **Then** --branch wins (warn).
- **Given** --base 25.3, **When** passed, **Then** branch is created from `if/stable/25.3` (uses repos default_base prefix logic).
- **Given** --no-fetch, **When** passed, **Then** no `git fetch` is run before branch creation.
- **Given** tag already exists in registry, **When** run, **Then** error DuplicateTag with hint.

## Files
- src/cli/new.rs — handler.
- src/git/shell_backend.rs — branch_create_from helper (or use gix-ref via gix_backend).

## Test Requirements
- Integration: 5 tests covering each AC.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-4i5.1, grove-ktk.4

## Notes
02-cli-ux.md new command section. Issue prefix comes from RepoEntry.issue_prefix (per-repo).

title: grove fork command
description:
## Context
`grove fork [source] <new_tag>` forks a new worktree from an existing projects branch. 1 or 2 positionals; one-arg form infers source from cwd.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** cwd inside project foo, **When** `grove fork bar` is run, **Then** source=foo, new tag=bar, new branch derived from foos branch.
- **Given** `grove fork foo bar`, **When** run, **Then** source=foo explicitly.
- **Given** 1 positional and cwd outside any project, **When** run, **Then** error with hint to pass source explicitly.
- **Given** 3+ positionals, **When** run, **Then** clap rejects with usage error.

## Files
- src/cli/fork.rs — handler with `args: Vec<String>` + length validation.

## Test Requirements
- Integration: 4 tests covering each AC.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-4i5.1, grove-ktk.4

## Notes
02-cli-ux.md fork section. Clap derive cannot natively express "1 or 2 positionals"; accept `Vec<String>` and validate in handler. Issue/branch flags same as `new`.

title: grove adopt command
description:
## Context
`grove adopt <tag> <path> [--move] [--issue N] [--base ref]` imports an existing worktree into the registry. With --move, relocates the directory into the repos work_dir first.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** an existing worktree at /tmp/foo and `grove adopt foo /tmp/foo`, **When** run, **Then** the project is added to the registry pointing at /tmp/foo.
- **Given** --move, **When** run, **Then** the directory is moved to `<work_dir>/<tag>` first, then registered.
- **Given** path is not a git worktree, **When** run, **Then** error WorktreeInvalid.
- **Given** tag already exists, **When** run, **Then** error DuplicateTag.

## Files
- src/cli/adopt.rs — handler.

## Test Requirements
- Integration: 4 tests covering each AC.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-4i5.1, grove-ktk.4

## Notes
Move uses fs::rename when same filesystem, falls back to copy+delete cross-fs. Verify worktree by checking `<path>/.git` exists and is a file (worktree marker).

title: grove rename command
description:
## Context
`grove rename <old> <new> [--no-move]` renames a project tag. By default also moves the directory; --no-move keeps the path unchanged.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** project foo at <work_dir>/foo, **When** `grove rename foo bar` is run, **Then** registry key changes to bar AND directory is moved to <work_dir>/bar AND `git worktree move` is invoked to keep gits worktree links correct.
- **Given** --no-move, **When** run, **Then** only the registry key changes; path stays.
- **Given** new tag already exists, **When** run, **Then** error DuplicateTag.

## Files
- src/cli/rename.rs — handler.

## Test Requirements
- Integration: 3 tests covering each AC.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-4i5.1, grove-ktk.4

## Notes
Order of operations matters: `git worktree move` before fs rename so git records the move correctly.

title: grove freeze / grove thaw commands
description:
## Context
Freeze toggles the `frozen` flag on a project. Frozen projects are excluded from `grove launch`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** `grove freeze <tag>`, **When** run, **Then** registry entry has frozen=true.
- **Given** `grove thaw <tag>`, **When** run, **Then** registry entry has frozen=false.
- **Given** no tag and cwd inside a project, **When** run, **Then** the cwds project is targeted.
- **Given** unknown tag, **When** run, **Then** error with near-match hint.

## Files
- src/cli/freeze.rs — handler covering both freeze and thaw.

## Test Requirements
- Unit: 4 tests.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-ktk.4

## Notes
02-cli-ux.md freeze section. No git op required; pure registry edit.

title: grove done command with safety checks
description:
## Context
'grove done <tag> [--force] [--keep-local] [--keep-remote]' removes a worktree. Refuses when uncommitted or unpushed work exists unless overridden.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** a clean fully-synced worktree, **When** the command runs, **Then** the worktree is removed via git worktree remove, the local branch is deleted, the remote tracking branch is deleted, and the project is removed from the registry.
- **Given** dirty work in the worktree, **When** run without the override flag, **Then** error with hint mentioning the override.
- **Given** unsynced commits, **When** run without the override flag, **Then** error with hint mentioning the override.
- **Given** --keep-local, **When** run, **Then** the local branch is not deleted.
- **Given** --keep-remote, **When** run, **Then** the remote tracking branch is not deleted.
- **Given** override flag set, **When** run, **Then** all safety checks are bypassed.

## Files
- src/cli/done.rs — handler.
- src/git/status.rs — extend Status with is_pushed flag (gix-only, no shell).

## Test Requirements
- Integration: 6 tests covering each AC.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-4i5.1, grove-rez.2, grove-ktk.4

## Notes
02-cli-ux.md done section. Remote branch deletion uses git CLI (push delete syntax).

title: GitFixture test helper + Phase 3 e2e
description:
## Context
Shared test-helper that spins up real git repos in tempdir with N worktrees. Used by every integration test from Phase 2 onward.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** GitFixture::new(), **When** called, **Then** creates a tempdir with a bare repo plus main checkout, and can plant N worktrees on demand via .add_worktree(tag, branch).
- **Given** an integration test using GitFixture, **When** the test ends, **Then** the tempdir is cleaned up (tempfile drop).
- **Given** GitFixture::with_grove_registry(), **When** called, **Then** populates a .grove/registry.json so command handlers find the projects.

## Files
- tests/common/mod.rs — GitFixture, helpers.
- tests/phase3_e2e.rs — end-to-end: new -> adopt -> rename -> freeze -> done.

## Test Requirements
- 2 unit tests verifying creation + cleanup.
- 1 end-to-end test exercising the full Phase 3 lifecycle.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-4i5.2 through grove-4i5.7

## Notes
04-launch-test-ci.md section 4. Use std::process::Command for the bare/main repo init (gix bare repo init may be limited).

title: Epic: Phase 4 — Multi-repo support
description:
Implement repo management subcommands (repo add/list/remove/show/default) and cross-repo grove list grouping with current-cwd repo first. Tag disambiguation with strsim near-match hints.

See: 00-master-plan.md (Phase 4), 02-cli-ux.md

title: grove repo add command
description:
## Context
Register a new repo into the global manifest. `grove repo add <path> [--id <id>] [--issue-prefix <PFX>] [--upstream <remote>] [--fork <remote>] [--default-base <ref>] [--default]`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** a path to a valid git repo not yet registered, **When** `grove repo add <path>` runs, **Then** repos.json gains a new entry with auto-derived id (basename of path), the .grove/ directory is created under work_dir, and an empty per-repo registry.json is written.
- **Given** --default, **When** passed, **Then** repos.json default_repo is set to the new id.
- **Given** --issue-prefix DEV, **When** passed, **Then** the repo entry has issue_prefix="DEV"; without it, no prefix is stored (issue feature disabled for the repo).
- **Given** path is not a git repo, **When** run, **Then** error NotAGitRepo.
- **Given** id already exists, **When** run, **Then** error DuplicateRepoId.

## Files
- src/cli/repo.rs — add subcommand handler.

## Test Requirements
- Integration: 5 tests covering each AC.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-ktk.1

## Notes
02-cli-ux.md repo section. Verify git via gix discovery before writing.

title: grove repo list/show/remove/default
description:
## Context
Round out the repo subcommand family.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** `grove repo list`, **When** run, **Then** outputs a table of registered repos (id, path, default_base, issue_prefix, is_default flag) plus --json output.
- **Given** `grove repo show <id>`, **When** run, **Then** prints full repo entry plus a count of registered projects.
- **Given** `grove repo remove <id>`, **When** run with no projects in that repos registry, **Then** repos.json entry is removed; if projects exist, error RepoNotEmpty unless --force is passed.
- **Given** `grove repo default <id>`, **When** run, **Then** repos.json default_repo is set to <id>.
- **Given** unknown id, **When** run on any of these, **Then** error with near-match hint via strsim.

## Files
- src/cli/repo.rs — extend.

## Test Requirements
- Integration: 5 tests.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-4pe.1

## Notes
02-cli-ux.md repo section. Removing a repo never deletes the on-disk worktrees or projects — only the registration.

title: Cross-repo grove list grouping
description:
## Context
Multi-repo grove list: scan all registered repos in parallel, group by repo, render current-cwds repo first.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** 2 repos registered, **When** `grove list` is run from cwd unrelated to either, **Then** both repos sections appear in alphabetical order by id.
- **Given** cwd inside repo Bs work_dir, **When** `grove list` is run, **Then** repo B section appears first, then others alphabetically.
- **Given** --repo <id>, **When** passed, **Then** only that repos section is rendered (other repos not scanned).
- **Given** a repo has zero projects, **When** listed, **Then** an empty section with "(no projects)" placeholder is shown.

## Files
- src/cli/list.rs — extend with cross-repo dispatch.

## Test Requirements
- Snapshot test (insta) with 2-repo fixture + cwd in each + no-cwd-match cases.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-rez.3, grove-4pe.1

## Notes
02-cli-ux.md list section. Scan repos via rayon outer loop, projects via inner rayon — flatten or use scope.

title: Tag disambiguation + strsim hints
description:
## Context
With multiple repos, the same tag can exist in two repos. Disambiguation via --repo, qualified `<repo>/<tag>` syntax, or interactive prompt suggestion.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** tag foo exists in repos desktop and ifkb, **When** `grove cd foo` is run with no --repo and cwd outside both, **Then** error lists qualified candidates: "ambiguous; try desktop/foo or ifkb/foo".
- **Given** cwd inside repo X and tag foo exists in both X and Y, **When** `grove cd foo` is run, **Then** Xs foo wins (cwd-based disambiguation).
- **Given** `grove cd desktop/foo`, **When** run, **Then** the qualified form unambiguously selects foo in desktop.
- **Given** an unknown tag baz close to foo, **When** referenced, **Then** error includes "did you mean: foo?" via strsim::jaro_winkler with threshold 0.85.

## Files
- src/repo/mod.rs — extend tag resolution.
- src/error.rs — extend hint_for() with strsim suggestions.

## Test Requirements
- Unit: 4 tests covering each AC.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-ktk.4

## Notes
02-cli-ux.md disambiguation section.

title: Epic: Phase 0 — Scaffold
description:
## Success Criteria
- Cargo build green, CI workflow passes, dist-workspace.toml ready for release.

title: Scaffold oysteinkrog/grove repo
description:
## Context
Bootstrap the new public Rust repo that replaces the Python grove. First bead in the chain — everything else depends on this.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** an empty workspace, **When** scaffolding completes, **Then** the repo at oysteinkrog/grove builds with cargo build, has MIT/Apache-2.0 dual license, edition 2024, MSRV 1.80, and a CI workflow that runs cargo fmt + clippy -D warnings + cargo test green on the empty crate.
- **Given** the scaffolded repo, **When** cargo-dist init is run, **Then** a dist-workspace.toml is created with targets x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, x86_64-pc-windows-msvc, x86_64-apple-darwin, aarch64-apple-darwin, aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu.

## Files
- Cargo.toml — package metadata, edition 2024, rust-version 1.80
- LICENSE-MIT, LICENSE-APACHE — dual license
- .github/workflows/ci.yml — fmt + clippy + test on linux/macos/windows
- dist-workspace.toml — cargo-dist config (5 targets)
- src/main.rs — `fn main() { println!("grove"); }` placeholder
- README.md — one-paragraph stub
- .gitignore — target/, .DS_Store

## Test Requirements
- CI green on empty crate (fmt, clippy -D warnings, test).

## Dependencies
- Depends on: none (root bead)

## Notes
Plan: /c/users/oystein/grove-rewrite-plan/00-master-plan.md Phase 0.
Use `cargo dist init` after `cargo init --bin`. Dual-license follows Rust ecosystem convention.

title: Epic: Phase 1 — Schema, registry, migration
description:
Implement the hybrid multi-repo config schema (global repos.json + per-repo config.json + per-repo registry.json), RepoContext discovery, and one-shot auto-migration from the legacy single-repo layout with .pre-rust-migration backups.

See: /c/users/oystein/grove-rewrite-plan/00-master-plan.md (Phase 1), 01-architecture.md sections 2-4

title: Implement GlobalConfig (repos.json)
description:
## Context
Global manifest at ~/.config/grove/repos.json indexing every registered repo. Source of truth for cross-repo dispatch.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** a fresh empty config dir, **When** GlobalConfig::load is called, **Then** it returns a default empty manifest with schema_version=1 (no error).
- **Given** a valid repos.json on disk, **When** GlobalConfig::load is called, **Then** it round-trips identically through serde (load -> save -> load).
- **Given** an unknown field in repos.json, **When** loaded, **Then** the unknown field is silently ignored (no deny_unknown_fields) — forward compat.
- **Given** a newer schema_version than supported, **When** loaded, **Then** returns a hard error with a hint to upgrade grove.

## Files
- src/config/global.rs — `ReposManifest { schema_version: u32, default_repo: Option<String>, repos: BTreeMap<String, RepoEntry> }`, `RepoEntry { main_repo: PathBuf, work_dir: PathBuf, dir_prefix: String, upstream_remote: String, fork_remote: String, default_base: String, issue_prefix: Option<String>, launch: Option<LaunchOverride> }`.
- src/config/mod.rs — re-exports.
- tests/config_round_trip.rs — load/save/load identity test.

## Test Requirements
- Unit: 4 tests (default, round-trip, unknown field, version mismatch).
- Integration: 1 golden-file test using tempdir.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-fut.1

## Notes
01-architecture.md section 2 has the full schema. Use serde_json. Path field uses PathBuf; serialize as string with serde feature.

title: Implement RepoConfig (per-repo overrides)
description:
## Context
Per-repo `<work_dir>/.grove/config.json` lets a repo override global launch settings. Optional file — absence means "inherit globals".

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** no per-repo config file, **When** RepoConfig::load is called, **Then** returns Ok(None) (not error).
- **Given** a valid per-repo config with launch overrides, **When** merged with global, **Then** per-repo fields take precedence and missing per-repo fields fall back to global.
- **Given** a per-repo config, **When** round-tripped through serde, **Then** identical bytes (modulo whitespace).

## Files
- src/config/repo.rs — `PerRepoConfig { schema_version: u32, launch: Option<LaunchOverride>, default_base: Option<String> }`, `LaunchOverride { terminal: Option<String>, wezterm_path: Option<PathBuf>, shell_command: Option<String> }`.
- src/config/mod.rs — `fn merge(global: &ReposManifest, repo_id: &str, per_repo: Option<&PerRepoConfig>) -> ResolvedConfig`.

## Test Requirements
- Unit: 3 tests (missing file, round-trip, merge precedence).

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-fut.1

## Notes
01-architecture.md section 2b. Merge is a pure function; test exhaustively.

title: Implement Registry (per-repo registry.json)
description:
## Context
Per-repo `<work_dir>/.grove/registry.json` holds the project list (tag -> Project). One registry per repo.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** a fresh repo with no registry, **When** Registry::load is called, **Then** returns default empty registry with schema_version=1.
- **Given** a registry with N projects, **When** round-tripped, **Then** project count and field values are preserved exactly (including timestamps, issue, frozen flag).
- **Given** a project with duplicate tag, **When** Registry::insert is called, **Then** returns Err(GroveError::DuplicateTag).
- **Given** an existing registry, **When** save is called, **Then** the file is written atomically (tmpfile + rename), and a single .bak rolling backup exists.

## Files
- src/registry/mod.rs — `Registry { schema_version: u32, projects: BTreeMap<String, Project> }`, load/save/insert/remove/rename/list.
- src/registry/project.rs — `Project { path: PathBuf, branch: String, base: String, created: OffsetDateTime, issue: Option<u32>, frozen: bool }`.

## Test Requirements
- Unit: 5 tests (default, round-trip, duplicate, atomic save, .bak rotation).

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-fut.1

## Notes
01-architecture.md section 2c. Use `time` crate (not chrono) for timestamps. OffsetDateTime with serde_with::rfc3339 for stable serialization.

title: Implement RepoContext + discovery
description:
## Context
RepoContext is the resolved union of GlobalConfig + RepoConfig + Registry for one repo. All command handlers receive a RepoContext. Discovery picks which repo applies based on cwd.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** cwd inside a registered repo work_dir, **When** RepoContext::discover() runs, **Then** that repo is selected without ambiguity.
- **Given** cwd unrelated to any repo and a default_repo set in globals, **When** discover runs, **Then** the default repo is selected.
- **Given** cwd unrelated and no default_repo, **When** discover runs, **Then** returns Err with a hint listing available repos.
- **Given** $GROVE_ORIG_CWD set, **When** discover runs, **Then** it walks up from $GROVE_ORIG_CWD (not from std::env::current_dir).
- **Given** cwd under exactly one repos work_dir, **When** discover runs, **Then** that repo wins even if its tag is ambiguous globally.

## Files
- src/repo/mod.rs — `RepoContext { id: String, global: ReposManifest, resolved: ResolvedConfig, registry: Registry }`, `pub fn discover(args: &Cli) -> Result<RepoContext>`.

## Test Requirements
- Unit: 6 tests covering each discovery branch.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-ktk.1, grove-ktk.2, grove-ktk.3

## Notes
01-architecture.md section 3. Discovery order: $GROVE_ORIG_CWD walk-up -> work_dir prefix match -> default_repo -> error. --repo <id> CLI flag short-circuits all of this.

title: Legacy config migration with backups
description:
## Context
On first run of the new binary, detect legacy ~/.config/grove/{config,registry}.json and migrate to the new hybrid layout. Backups go in .pre-rust-migration siblings.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** legacy config.json and registry.json present and no repos.json, **When** migrate::run() is called, **Then** it creates repos.json with one repo entry (id "desktop", issue_prefix "DESKTOP") and writes per-repo .grove/{config,registry}.json under the legacy work_dir.
- **Given** migration runs, **When** it completes successfully, **Then** the originals are renamed to config.json.pre-rust-migration and registry.json.pre-rust-migration (preserved, not deleted).
- **Given** repos.json already exists, **When** migrate::run() is called, **Then** it is a no-op (returns Ok early).
- **Given** legacy registry has N projects, **When** migrated, **Then** the new per-repo registry contains exactly the same N projects with identical fields (modulo timestamp normalization to RFC3339).

## Files
- src/migrate/mod.rs — `pub fn run_if_needed(config_dir: &Path) -> Result<MigrationOutcome>`.
- src/main.rs — call run_if_needed before command dispatch.

## Test Requirements
- Unit: 4 tests (legacy present, already migrated, partial legacy state, malformed legacy).
- Integration: 1 golden-file test using Oysteins real config copied into tempdir (next bead).

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-ktk.1, grove-ktk.2, grove-ktk.3

## Notes
01-architecture.md section 4. Legacy timestamps are naive — interpret as local time, normalize to UTC RFC3339.

title: Validation gate: migrate Oysteins real config
description:
## Context
Validation gate for Phase 1. Run the migration against a copy of Oysteins real config to prove no project data is lost.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** /c/users/oystein/.config/grove/{config,registry}.json copied into a tempdir, **When** migrate::run_if_needed runs against the tempdir, **Then** the resulting repos.json + per-repo registry contains exactly the same project tags (set equality) and per-project field values (branch, base, issue, frozen, path) as the legacy registry.
- **Given** the migration result, **When** re-run, **Then** it is a no-op (idempotency).

## Files
- tests/migrate_real_config.rs — copies real config into tempdir, runs migration, asserts.

## Test Requirements
- Integration: 1 test, must pass.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-ktk.5

## Notes
Use #[ignore] gate the test if running outside Oysteins machine. Path: /c/users/oystein/.config/grove/.

title: Epic: Phase 2 — Read-only commands
description:
Implement list, path, cd, status commands using gix-backed worktree inspection and rayon parallel status scanning. Snapshot tests for table/JSON output.

See: 00-master-plan.md (Phase 2), 02-cli-ux.md, 03-git-internals.md

title: WorktreeManager trait + gix backend skeleton
description:
## Context
Define a trait WorktreeManager so worktree mutations and inspection can swap backends. First impl is gix (inspect-only operations: list, find, status). Mutations (add/remove/move) come later via shell backend (Phase 3).

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** a bare or working repo path, **When** GixBackend::list_worktrees runs, **Then** it returns the same set as `git worktree list --porcelain` (asserted via integration test against a real repo).
- **Given** a worktree path, **When** GixBackend::open is called, **Then** it returns a handle exposing branch name and HEAD oid via gix.
- **Given** a missing path, **When** open is called, **Then** returns Err(GroveError::WorktreeNotFound(path)).

## Files
- src/git/mod.rs — `pub trait WorktreeManager { fn list(&self, main: &Path) -> Result<Vec<WorktreeInfo>>; fn open(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<Worktree>; }`.
- src/git/gix_backend.rs — GixBackend impl.
- Cargo.toml — add gix with minimal features.

## Test Requirements
- Unit: trait shape compiles.
- Integration: list + open against tempdir-created repo with 2 worktrees.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-fut.1

## Notes
03-git-internals.md section 2-3. Use gix 0.83+. Disable default features; enable only worktree, status, revwalk as needed.

title: gix status detection (dirty/ahead/behind)
description:
## Context
Compute per-worktree status using gix: is_dirty, ahead, behind, untracked count. Powers grove list and grove status.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** a clean worktree, **When** status is computed, **Then** dirty=false, ahead=0, behind=0, untracked=0.
- **Given** a worktree with modified tracked file, **When** status is computed, **Then** dirty=true.
- **Given** a worktree whose branch is 2 commits ahead of its upstream, **When** status is computed, **Then** ahead=2, behind=0.
- **Given** a worktree with no upstream, **When** status is computed, **Then** ahead/behind are None (not 0).

## Files
- src/git/status.rs — `pub struct Status { dirty: bool, ahead: Option<u32>, behind: Option<u32>, untracked: u32 }`, `pub fn compute(wt: &Worktree) -> Result<Status>`.

## Test Requirements
- Unit + integration: 5 tests covering each branch above (tempdir + real git ops).

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-rez.1

## Notes
03-git-internals.md section 3. Use repo.is_dirty() + repo.rev_walk() with boundary for ahead/behind.

title: grove list table renderer (comfy-table)
description:
## Context
Default `grove list` output: table with columns Tag, Branch, Base, Status, Issue. Grouped by repo, current cwds repo first.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** N projects across M repos, **When** `grove list` is run, **Then** output has M sections (one per repo) with a header line and an N-row table per section.
- **Given** cwd inside repo X, **When** `grove list` is run, **Then** repo X is the first section regardless of alphabetical order.
- **Given** a frozen project, **When** listed, **Then** the tag is rendered in dim style and shows (frozen) suffix.
- **Given** --repo <id>, **When** passed, **Then** only that repo section is rendered.

## Files
- src/cli/list.rs — handler.
- src/display.rs — comfy-table helpers, color rendering.

## Test Requirements
- Snapshot (insta) test for table output with 2 repos, 3 projects each.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-ktk.4, grove-rez.2

## Notes
02-cli-ux.md output spec. Use comfy-table; presets UTF8_FULL_CONDENSED. Respect NO_COLOR + is-terminal.

title: grove list --short and --json modes
description:
## Context
--short prints one line per project; --json emits versioned structured output.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** `grove list --short`, **When** run, **Then** each project is a single line `<repo>/<tag>  <branch>  <status-summary>` with consistent column padding.
- **Given** `grove list --json`, **When** run, **Then** output is `{"version": 1, "repos": [{"id": "...", "projects": [{...}]}]}` with stable field ordering and ends with a single trailing newline.
- **Given** --json --no-status, **When** run, **Then** the status sub-object is omitted (fast path skipping git scans).

## Files
- src/cli/list.rs — extend with format dispatch.
- src/display.rs — short renderer; serde structs for JSON output.

## Test Requirements
- Snapshot tests for --short and --json against fixture registry.
- JSON schema asserted via serde_json::Value parsing in test.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-rez.3

## Notes
02-cli-ux.md JSON schema. Version field is required for downstream tooling.

title: grove path + grove cd commands
description:
## Context
`grove path <tag>` prints the absolute path. `grove cd <tag>` is an alias (shells use it from a wrapper function). Both must be fast (no git scan).

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** a known tag, **When** `grove path <tag>` is run, **Then** stdout is exactly the worktree absolute path with no trailing whitespace or newline beyond a single LF.
- **Given** an unknown tag, **When** run, **Then** exit code is non-zero and stderr suggests near matches via strsim (placeholder if Phase 4 not done — fall back to plain "unknown tag" error).
- **Given** ambiguous tag across repos, **When** no --repo flag, **Then** error lists candidate `<repo>/<tag>` qualified forms.
- **Given** `grove repo path --default`, **When** run, **Then** prints the default repos work_dir (for fish wrapper fallback cd).

## Files
- src/cli/path.rs — handler.
- src/cli/cd.rs — alias dispatcher.
- src/cli/repo.rs — `grove repo path` (added here, expanded in Phase 4).

## Test Requirements
- Integration: 4 tests covering each AC.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-ktk.4

## Notes
02-cli-ux.md cd/path section. Output must be parseable by shells (single line, no decoration).

title: grove status command (per-project detail)
description:
## Context
Deep status for a single project: branch, base, ahead/behind, dirty files list, unmerged commits, age.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** `grove status <tag>`, **When** run, **Then** prints a multi-section report including head branch, upstream, ahead/behind, dirty file list (truncated to 10 with count), age since last commit.
- **Given** --json, **When** run, **Then** emits versioned JSON with the same fields.
- **Given** an invalid tag, **When** run, **Then** error with near-match hint.

## Files
- src/cli/status.rs — handler.
- src/git/status.rs — extend with detail variants.

## Test Requirements
- Snapshot test (insta) for human + JSON output on a fixture worktree.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-rez.2, grove-ktk.4

## Notes
02-cli-ux.md status section. Reuse Status compute from rez.2.

title: rayon parallel status scanning
description:
## Context
Scanning N projects sequentially is slow. Use rayon to fan out git status calls across all CPU cores.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** 10 worktrees, **When** statuses are computed, **Then** wall time is at most 2x the slowest single scan (not 10x).
- **Given** rayon parallelism, **When** a single scan errors, **Then** the error is returned and other scans are not silently dropped (collected into a Vec<Result<_>>).
- **Given** RAYON_NUM_THREADS=1, **When** scans run, **Then** behavior is identical to sequential (sanity).

## Files
- src/git/status.rs — `pub fn compute_all(worktrees: &[Worktree]) -> Vec<Result<Status>>`.

## Test Requirements
- Bench: ad-hoc cargo bench or criterion micro-bench documenting speedup.
- Unit: error-propagation test.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-rez.2

## Notes
03-git-internals.md section 6. Rayon, not tokio. par_iter().map(compute).collect().

title: Snapshot tests for read-only output
description:
## Context
Lock down list/status output formats with insta snapshots so future changes are deliberate.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** a fixture registry with 2 repos and 5 projects in mixed states (clean, dirty, ahead, frozen), **When** `grove list`, `grove list --short`, `grove list --json`, `grove status <tag>`, `grove status <tag> --json` are run, **Then** outputs match committed insta snapshots byte-for-byte.
- **Given** NO_COLOR=1 in env, **When** any output runs, **Then** no ANSI escape codes appear in output.

## Files
- tests/snapshots_readonly.rs — drives commands via assert_cmd.
- tests/snapshots/ — insta snapshot files.

## Test Requirements
- Integration: 6 snapshot tests.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-rez.3, grove-rez.4, grove-rez.6

## Notes
Use insta, assert_cmd, tempfile. Fixture registry built by tests/common/mod.rs.

title: Epic: Phase 5 — Launch
description:
Implement grove launch with Terminal trait abstraction, WindowsTerminal + Wezterm impls, WSL <-> Windows path bridging in src/paths.rs, autodetection via WEZTERM_PANE/WT_SESSION env vars and PATH probe.

See: 00-master-plan.md (Phase 5), 04-launch-test-ci.md

title: WSL to Windows path bridging in paths.rs
description:
## Context
Launching Windows terminals from WSL requires the WSL-style path /c/work/foo to become the Windows path C colon backslash work backslash foo. Pure-Rust function (no wslpath shell-out) so it works in CI on Linux/macOS without WSL.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** a /c/work/foo input, **When** to_windows_path runs, **Then** the result is the Windows form with drive letter C and backslash separators.
- **Given** a /mnt/c/work/foo input, **When** called, **Then** the result is the same Windows form.
- **Given** an already-Windows input, **When** called, **Then** returns it unchanged.
- **Given** /home/oystein, **When** called, **Then** returns the original path (no rewrite when there is no drive prefix).
- **Given** a UNC-style //wsl share path, **When** called, **Then** returns it unchanged.

## Files
- src/paths.rs — to_windows_path and to_wsl_path functions.

## Test Requirements
- Unit: 6 tests covering each AC.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-fut.1

## Notes
04-launch-test-ci.md section 3. Use a simple regex or manual prefix check, no external dep.

title: Terminal trait + autodetection
description:
## Context
Abstract terminal launcher behind a trait so different terminals (wt, wezterm, future tmux/iTerm) plug in. Autodetect preferred terminal from env vars and PATH.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** WEZTERM_PANE set in env, **When** autodetect runs, **Then** returns Terminal::Wezterm.
- **Given** WT_SESSION set, **When** autodetect runs, **Then** returns Terminal::WindowsTerminal.
- **Given** neither env var set and wt.exe on PATH, **When** autodetect runs, **Then** returns WindowsTerminal.
- **Given** config explicit terminal override, **When** autodetect runs, **Then** the override wins regardless of env.
- **Given** no terminal available, **When** autodetect runs, **Then** returns an error variant NoTerminalAvailable.

## Files
- src/launch/mod.rs — Terminal trait with launch and dry_run methods, plus autodetect function.

## Test Requirements
- Unit: 5 tests via env-var manipulation (use temp_env crate or set-then-restore).

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-fut.1

## Notes
04-launch-test-ci.md section 2.

title: WindowsTerminal impl (wt.exe)
description:
## Context
Concrete Terminal impl that spawns one tab per project via Windows Terminal CLI (wt.exe).

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** 3 tabs, **When** WindowsTerminal launch is called, **Then** a single wt.exe invocation is built using new-tab separators (one wt process, not three).
- **Given** a tab cwd of /c/work/foo, **When** launching, **Then** wt receives the startingDirectory argument in Windows path form.
- **Given** a tab title, **When** launching, **Then** wt receives the title argument.
- **Given** dry_run, **When** called, **Then** returns the constructed argv as a Vec of String without spawning.

## Files
- src/launch/windows_terminal.rs — WindowsTerminal struct and impl.

## Test Requirements
- Unit: 4 tests asserting argv construction via dry_run.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-u6c.2, grove-u6c.1

## Notes
04-launch-test-ci.md section 2. wt.exe is reachable from WSL via /init resolution (or full Windows path).

title: Wezterm impl
description:
## Context
Concrete Terminal impl that uses wezterm cli spawn to open a tab in an existing wezterm session.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** 3 tabs in a running wezterm session, **When** Wezterm launch is called, **Then** 3 separate wezterm-cli spawn invocations run (wezterm cli does not support multi-tab in one command).
- **Given** a tab cwd, **When** launching, **Then** the path is converted to Windows form before being passed to wezterm.exe.
- **Given** wezterm_path is configured, **When** launching, **Then** that path is used; otherwise wezterm is looked up on PATH.
- **Given** dry_run, **When** called, **Then** returns the argv list (one entry per spawn).

## Files
- src/launch/wezterm.rs — Wezterm struct and impl.

## Test Requirements
- Unit: 4 tests via dry_run.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-u6c.2, grove-u6c.1

## Notes
04-launch-test-ci.md section 2.

title: grove launch command + mock-terminal tests
description:
## Context
The grove launch subcommand: filter non-frozen projects, build Tab descriptors, hand to the autodetected (or explicit) Terminal impl.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** N non-frozen projects, **When** grove launch is run, **Then** Tab descriptors are built alphabetically by tag (one per project), each with title equal to tag, cwd equal to project path, and the configured shell command.
- **Given** --only foo,bar, **When** passed, **Then** only Tabs for foo and bar are built (frozen filter still applies).
- **Given** --dry-run, **When** passed, **Then** the Terminal dry_run output is printed and no subprocess is spawned.
- **Given** --no-claude, **When** passed, **Then** the per-tab command is just fish login shell with no claude invocation.
- **Given** --terminal wt, **When** passed, **Then** that Terminal impl is used overriding autodetect.

## Files
- src/cli/launch.rs — handler.

## Test Requirements
- Integration: 5 tests using a MockTerminal that captures Tabs into a Vec.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: grove-u6c.3, grove-u6c.4, grove-ktk.4

## Notes
04-launch-test-ci.md section 1.
